name: rag-pipeline
description: Details on the Retrieval Augmented Generation pipeline, Ingestion, and Vector Search.
capabilities: Scan and process documents from a docs directory
Clean markdown files by removing frontmatter and imports
Chunk text into segments with configurable overlap
Store and search vectors in Qdrant collection
Create personalized prompts based on user background
author: AbdulSamad94
securitySummary: All static findings are FALSE POSITIVES. The scanned files are pure documentation: SKILL.md contains markdown explaining RAG architecture patterns, and skill-report.json contains metadata. No executable code, scripts, or network calls exist. The scanner incorrectly flagged path references and metadata fields as security issues.
valueStatement: Users struggle to connect document repositories with AI models for accurate, context-aware responses. This skill provides a complete RAG pipeline including document ingestion, vector search with Qdrant, and personalized prompt generation for specialized knowledge bases.
userTitle: Build RAG Pipelines with Vector Search
limitations: Does not include the actual Python implementation files
Requires external Qdrant vector database setup
Requires Google Vertex AI or compatible embedding service
Does not handle real-time document updates automatically

useCases.title: Build Knowledge Bases
useCases.description: Create searchable vector databases from documentation for enterprise knowledge management systems

useCases.title: Enhance LLM Responses
useCases.description: Ground AI model responses in proprietary documents for accurate, context-aware answers

useCases.title: Document Search Systems
useCases.description: Enable semantic search across documentation sets using vector similarity

promptTemplates.title: Basic RAG Query
promptTemplates.prompt: Search the documentation for information about [topic] and provide relevant excerpts

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Document Search
promptTemplates.prompt: Find all references to [concept] across the documentation and summarize findings

promptTemplates.title: Context-Aware Answer
promptTemplates.prompt: Based on the retrieved context about [topic], explain how to implement this feature

promptTemplates.title: Personalized Response
promptTemplates.scenario: Use user background for tailored answers
promptTemplates.prompt: Answer my question about [topic] using the documentation, considering my background in [field]

bestPractices: Use consistent chunk sizes and overlap to ensure relevant context is captured
Clean documents thoroughly before embedding to remove noise and metadata
Test vector search quality with sample queries before deployment
antiPatterns: Using very large chunk sizes that include irrelevant context
Skipping document cleaning steps which can pollute embeddings
Ignoring similarity metrics when evaluating search quality

faq.question: What embedding models are supported?
faq.answer: The pipeline uses text-embedding-004 by default. Any model producing 768-dimensional vectors with cosine similarity is compatible.

faq.question: What is the recommended chunk size?
faq.answer: Default is 1000 characters with 100-character overlap. Adjust based on document structure and query complexity.

faq.question: How do I integrate with existing systems?
faq.answer: The modular architecture allows replacing individual components. The ingestion script and vector search client can be imported as modules.

faq.question: Is my data safe during processing?
faq.answer: Documents are processed locally before embedding. Only vector representations are stored in Qdrant, not raw content.

faq.question: Why are my search results poor?
faq.answer: Check chunk size settings, document cleaning quality, and embedding model compatibility. Consider adding more overlap or re-chunking.

faq.question: How does this compare to other RAG tools?
faq.answer: This implementation provides full control over each pipeline stage. Unlike managed services, you control infrastructure and data flow.
